channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey struck suspected Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day on Thursday following an attack on the premises of a key defense company which killed at least five people, the state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Indonesia says its coast guard drove away Chinese ship that interrupted survey in disputed sea
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian patrol ships drove a Chinese coast guard vessel away from a survey vessel in a disputed area of the South China Sea for the second time in three days, Indonesian authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin presides over BRICS summit seeking to expand Russia's clout
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has presided over a closing session of a summit of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, hailing its role as a counterbalance to the West.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian lawmakers ratify pact with North Korea as US confirms that Pyongyang sent troops to Russia
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian lawmakers on Thursday ratified a pact with North Korea envisioning mutual military assistance, a move that comes as the U.S. confirmed the deployment of 3,000 North Korean troops to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Well-known Cambodian investigative journalist released on bail
item.description: A Cambodian investigative reporter who was charged with a criminal offense after exposing online scams and corruption has been released on bail.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Climate-threatened Pacific Islands criticize Australia at Commonwealth meeting
item.description: APIA, Samoa (AP) — Several Pacific island nations singled out Australia to do more to phase out fossil fuel exports during the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting which opened in Samoa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president raises possibility of supplying Ukraine with arms
item.description: South Korea’s president has raised the possibility of supplying Ukraine with weapons while stressing that his government “won’t sit idle” as North Korea allegedly sends troops to support Russia’s aggression toward its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man pleads guilty to displaying a Nazi symbol at pro-Palestinian rally
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — A restaurateur pleaded guilty Thursday to displaying a Nazi symbol during a pro-Palestinian rally in Sydney in early October. Alan Yazbek was photographed at the Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive displacement from Israel-Hezbollah war transforms Beirut’s famed commercial street
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Inside what was once one of Beirut’s oldest and best-known cinemas, dozens of Lebanese, Palestinians and Syrians displaced by the Israel-Hezbollah war spend their time following the news on their phones, cooking, chatting and walking around to pass the time.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron says France will provide 100 million-euro aid package to support Lebanon
item.description: France will provide a 100-million euro, or $108 million aid package to support Lebanon, French President Emmanuel Macron told an international conference Thursday in Paris.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical storm battering Philippines leaves at least 24 people dead in flooding and landslides
item.description: Widespread flooding and landslides set off by a tropical storm in the northeastern Philippines left at least 24 people dead, swept away cars and prompted authorities to scramble for motorboats to rescue trapped villagers, some on roofs.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trash carried by a North Korean balloon again falls on the presidential compound in Seoul
item.description: South Korea says trash carried by a North Korean balloon has fallen on the presidential compound in central Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard officer charged in plot to kill American author in US
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment against four Iranian officials Tuesday for their alleged involvement in a murder-for-hire targeting Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine blasts UN chief over potential meeting with Putin at BRICS, says stayed away from Kyiv's peace summit
item.description: Ukraine blasted U.N. Secretary-General Guterres for allegedly agreeing to meet with Russian President Putin during the BRICS summit as leaders from the West's top adversaries like China and Iran convene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah naval base in Beirut as Iran rallies Gulf Arab nations
item.description: Hezbollah launched missiles on central Israel Tuesday. Meanwhile, Israel Defense Forces said it struck a Hezbollah naval base in Beirut, along with other terrorist targets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing student in Spain was likely crushed, incinerated after passing out in trash while partying: cops
item.description: A student missing in Majorca, Spain, was likely crushed to death in a garbage truck before she was incinerated at a waste disposal plant, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gunmen kill 7 people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Gunmen fatally shot at least seven people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir and injured at least five others, officials said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Italian surfer dies in freak accident after swordfish impales her while surfing in Indonesia
item.description: An Italian surfer hunting for waves off the west coast of Sumatra died after she was stabbed in the chest by the bill of a swordfish, in what is being considered a freak accident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voting from abroad: What to know
item.description: Most U.S. citizens who reside outside the country can vote absentee in primary and general elections.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Austin warns 'no silver bullet' to defeat Putin as US aid hangs in balance ahead of elections
item.description: Secretary of Defense Austin said there is "no silver bullet" to defeat Russian President Putin and urged the global community to bolster aid to Ukraine as concern mounts over continued U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats urge Biden to push Israel for press freedom in Gaza
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are pressing the Biden administration to get tougher on Israel when it comes to press freedoms in Gaza, warning that a lack of media access in the war-torn territory has stifled any push for accountability surrounding military operations that have killed tens of thousands o
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Funeral home in Poland apologizes after a corpse falls out of a hearse in traffic
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A funeral home in Poland issued an unusual apology on Saturday after a corpse that it was transporting fell out of a hearse and into traffic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah vows a new phase in the war, as Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Sinwar
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah vowed to launch a new phase of fighting against invading Israeli troops Friday, a day after Israel said its forces in the Palestinian enclave of Gaza had killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, the chief architect of last year’s brutal attack on Israe
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WFLA, NBC
